evals.prompt: Can you help me improve this prompt: "summarise this document"
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis of 2+ issues, improved prompt with role + XML tags + output constraints, What Changed section explaining each improvement
expectations: Output contains all three sections: Diagnosis, Improved Prompt, and What Changed
Diagnosis has at least 2 specific bullet points identifying distinct issues
Improved prompt includes a role definition (e.g. 'You are a...')
Improved prompt uses at least one XML tag for structure (e.g. <document>, <system>, <instructions>)
Improved prompt specifies an output length, format, or audience constraint
What Changed section explains the reasoning (why) for at least one change

evals.prompt: Can you help me improve this prompt: "You are a helpful assistant. Help me with customer support emails. Be professional and concise."
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis identifying vague role/no context/no output format, improved prompt with context block + numbered instructions + worked example, What Changed explaining the why
expectations: Output contains all three sections: Diagnosis, Improved Prompt, and What Changed
Diagnosis identifies missing context or vague role as a specific weakness
Improved prompt includes a context block or placeholder for company/product information
Improved prompt includes a worked example in <example> or <examples> tags
Improved prompt uses numbered or explicitly structured instructions
What Changed explains why adding an example improves the prompt

evals.prompt: Can you help me improve this prompt: "You are an AI agent. You can browse the web, read files, and run code to help users. Do whatever the user asks."
evals.expected_output: Diagnosis flagging unsafe 'do whatever' instruction, improved prompt with tool_use block and safety block, What Changed explaining agentic-specific additions
expectations: Output contains all three sections: Diagnosis, Improved Prompt, and What Changed
Diagnosis explicitly flags 'do whatever the user asks' or unconstrained obedience as unsafe or problematic
Improved prompt includes a <tool_use> block specifying parallel and sequential tool call guidance
Improved prompt includes a <safety> block requiring user confirmation before destructive or irreversible actions
The phrase 'do whatever' does NOT appear in the improved prompt
What Changed mentions the safety block and explains why it is needed for agentic prompts
